data.title: HIVE Digital mines 102 BTC in January, Bitcoin holdings up by 34% Y/Y
data.description:
• HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) mined 102 BTC in January, bringing its HODL portfolio to 2,657 BTC, valued at $271 million as of January 31, the company said on Thursday.
  • The company's Bitcoin holdings increased by 34% year-over-year.
  • However, on a month-over-month basis, BTC holdings saw a decline as HIVE strategically sold a portion of its BTC to invest in capital assets, including Bitfarms' (BITF) 200 megawatt ("MW") hydro-powered Bitcoin mining assets in Paraguay.
  • HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) achieved an average daily production rate of 3.3 BTC, equivalent to 18 BTC per Exahash.

data.title: Asia-Pacific markets rise, tracking gains on Wall Street amid cooling US-China trade tensions
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly in rise on Thursday tracking gains on Wall Street and driven by easing concerns over a global trade war, after the US and China adopted more restrained tariff measures, with US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping scheduled for a phone call soon.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.58% to surpass 39,000, while the broader Topix Index rose 0.7% to 2,764 on Thursday, marking a third consecutive session of gains for Japanese stocks. The Japanese yen strengthened beyond 152 per dollar on Thursday, reaching its highest level in eight weeks, as Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura said that the central bank must lift the policy rate to at least 1% in the latter part of fiscal 2025.

Meanwhile, investors kept a close eye on the failed merger talks between Honda and Nissan, with Honda proposing an acquisition of Nissan instead.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.83% to around 3,235 while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.6% to 10,225 on Thursday, recouping some losses from the previous session, with artificial intelligence and related technology stocks leading the charge.

Those gains were driven by enthusiasm around Chinese artificial intelligence startup DeepSeek, which is poised to challenge Western AI leaders. The offshore yuan steadied round 7.28 per dollar, as concerns over escalating U.S.-China trade tensions began to subside.

Investors are now focused on China's upcoming inflation data, which will offer further insights into the country's economic health.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.61% to 20,690 on Thursday morning session, shifting from notable losses in the previous session as all sectors moved higher.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.22% to 78,094 in morning trade on Thursday, retreating for the second straight session, weighed down by consumer durables, real estate, and metals.

The Indian rupee (USD:INR) hit an all-time low of 87.54 on Thursday amid increased USD buying, driven by the maturity of non-deliverable forward positions, capital outflows, and investor risk aversion.

Traders remained cautious, although the RBI is expected to deliver an interest rate cut amid a slowdown in economic growth.

Australia (AS51) rose 1.23% to close at 8,521 on Thursday, recovering most of its earlier losses for the week as concerns over a global trade war eased. The Australian dollar held steady around $0.628 on Thursday, after rising for three consecutive sessions, supported by a weaker US dollar.

Meanwhile, data revealed that Australia’s trade surplus narrowed in December, as export growth slowed while imports rose at a faster pace.

In the U.S., on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher, buoyed by a sharp drop in long-term Treasury yields as investors digested a mix of earnings reports and economic data.

U.S. stock futures rise on Thursday following gains on Wall Street overnight during a week plagued by tariffs, disappointing tech earnings, and mixed US economic data: Dow +0.22%; S&P 500 +0.26%; Nasdaq +0.30%.

Looking ahead, investors are focusing on quarterly reports from Amazon and other major companies on Thursday, as well as the latest weekly jobless claims.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

  • China’s services sector growth slows to four-month low in January

  • Japan’s Jan services PMI revised upward to 53; real wages rises for 2nd consecutive month

  • Trump in 'no hurry' to speak to China's Xi Jinping over escalating trade tensions

  • Tit-for-Tat: China hits back with tariffs on U.S. coal and LNG, unveils Google probe

  • U.S. will delay Canada tariffs as countries work on beefing up border security

data.title: ArcelorMittal misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• ArcelorMittal S.A. press release (NYSE:MT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.52 misses by $0.21.
  • Revenue of $14.71B (+1.1% Y/Y) misses by $440M.
  • Net debt at the end of 2024 was $5.1B (gross debt of $11.6B and cash and cash equivalents of $6.5B). Year-end 2024 liquidity totaled $12.0B.
  • Share repurchases driving enhanced value: During 2024, the company repurchased 52 million shares, reducing the total shares outstanding by 6.3% and bringing the fully diluted share count reduction since September 2020 to 37%.

  • Book value per share is $64/sh at the end of 2024.

  • Outlook: The company believes demand will increase during 2025: The company expects higher apparent demand in FY 2025 compared to FY 2024. With low inventory levels, especially in Europe, the company is optimistic that restocking activity will supplement real demand improvementCapex discipline in support of growth and competitive decarbonization: Capex in 2025 is projected to be within the range of $4.5-$5.0bn, including $1.4-$1.5bn on our strategic growth projects and $0.3-$0.4bn on projects related to decarbonizationPositive free cash flow outlook in 2025 and beyond: Cash flow in 2025 is expected to be supported by working capital optimization. The completion of the company’s strategic growth projects is expected to support structurally higher EBITDA and investable cash flow in the coming periods.

  • The Company expects world ex-China apparent steel consumption to grow by 2.5% to 3.5% in 2025 as compared to 2024, which is expected to support steel shipment growth in 2025. Expectations for ASC by region in 2025: Europe flat products to grow 0% to 2%; US flat products to grow 1% to 3%; China to remain stable; Brazil to be stable (following strong growth in 2024 of 8%) and India to grow 6% to 7%.

data.title: CERo Therapeutics to raise $5M via ATM offering
data.description:
Innovative immunotherapy company CERo Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CERO) priced its “reasonable best efforts” public offering with participation from a member of the company’s board and a single institutional investor for the purchase and sale of ~2.55M shares and warrants to purchase up to the same shares at a combined purchase price of $1.96.

The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about February 7, 2025.

The gross proceeds are expected to be ~$5M.

Net proceeds will be used for advancement of our clinical programs and working capital and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: PC Connection anticipates growth through AI and device refresh investments in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: PC Connection, Inc. (NASDAQ:CNXN) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Timothy McGrath highlighted that full-year revenue reached $2.8 billion, with gross profit increasing 1.6% and achieving a record gross margin of 18.6%. He noted that Q4 consolidated net sales grew 1.8%, driven by a 14% increase in notebook mobility and desktop revenue from PC refresh initiatives.
  • Advanced technologies revenue faced challenges due to delayed AI deployments and data center refreshes. However, McGrath expressed optimism about the growing pipeline of opportunities in this segment.
  • Public Sector Solutions delivered strong results, with Q4 net sales rising 42.9%, driven by increased federal and state/local government contracts, although at lower-than-average margins.
  • CFO Thomas Baker stated, "In the fourth quarter, SG&A increased by 5.2% over the prior year," attributing the rise to investments in sales and technical capabilities. He also announced a 50% increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.15 per share, payable on March 14, 2025.

Outlook

  • Management expects IT budgets to improve in 2025, supported by growth in data center and infrastructure projects, with AI and server consolidation as key drivers. McGrath emphasized the company's confidence in outperforming the US IT market growth rate by 200 basis points.
  • AI initiatives remain a focus, with advancements in the Helix platform and recognition from the Naval Information Warfare Center for AI innovation.
  • CFO Baker projected SG&A growth of around 3% to 4% year-over-year in the coming quarters, with a focus on operational efficiency and variable compensation tied to growth.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net income declined by 12.9% to $20.7 million, and earnings per share dropped 12.8% to $0.78, reflecting ongoing challenges in advanced technology revenue and product mix shifts.
  • Business Solutions segment net sales fell 3.7%, with a 12% decline in advanced technology sales partially offset by a 2% rise in endpoint device sales. Gross margins improved by 70 basis points to 23.9%, driven by customer mix and software sales.
  • Public Sector Solutions saw a 30.2% increase in gross profit, despite a margin decline of 150 basis points due to lower-margin project rollouts.
  • Enterprise Solutions experienced a 6.4% drop in net sales, largely due to a 28% decline in advanced technology sales, with gross margins decreasing by 50 basis points to 14.9%.

Q&A

  • Anthony Lebiedzinski, Sidoti, asked about sales trends and Q1 expectations. CFO Baker responded that October performed better than past years, but November was notably weak. He anticipated Q1 revenue to be flat or grow in low single digits, while McGrath added that the project funnel indicates significant opportunities in 2025.
  • Lebiedzinski inquired about vertical markets with growth potential. McGrath identified retail, healthcare, and manufacturing as key sectors, with SMB customers showing slower recovery due to economic concerns.
  • Lebiedzinski also asked about SG&A growth relative to gross profit. Baker clarified that investments in technical resources and tools are positioning the company for long-term growth, with some one-time Q4 expenses unlikely to recur.
  • Regarding tariffs, McGrath acknowledged supply chain complexities but emphasized proactive customer engagement to mitigate impacts.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on slow recovery in advanced technologies and SMB customer spending, while acknowledging positive investments in AI and technical capabilities.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, highlighting strategic investments and growth opportunities in 2025. However, responses to analyst questions reflected some caution, particularly regarding near-term revenue trends.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment appeared slightly more optimistic, with an emphasis on future growth drivers and AI opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted toward optimism for 2025, emphasizing AI-driven growth and data center investments, compared to prior cautious commentary on delayed IT spending recovery.
  • Management's tone indicated increased confidence in strategic initiatives, while analysts maintained a neutral-to-slightly-positive stance, focusing on near-term challenges in advanced technology revenue.
  • Key metrics showed weaker Q4 performance, with net income and EPS declining compared to Q3's record results, though investments in AI and technical capabilities were highlighted as transformative.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing challenges in advanced technology deployments, particularly delays in AI and data center projects.
  • Tariffs and supply chain uncertainties were flagged as potential headwinds, with efforts underway to optimize customer programs.
  • Analysts raised concerns about SMB recovery and the impact of higher SG&A on profitability, though management emphasized these as foundational investments for long-term growth.

Final Takeaway

PC Connection, Inc. navigated a challenging Q4 with strategic investments in AI capabilities, technical resources, and customer engagement tools, setting the stage for anticipated growth in 2025. While near-term performance was impacted by advanced technology delays and product mix shifts, the company remains optimistic about its ability to capitalize on AI-driven demand and outperform the IT market growth rate.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Patterson-UTI Energy misses top and bottom-line estimates
data.description:
• Patterson-UTI Energy press release (NASDAQ:PTEN): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.13 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $1.2B (-24.1% Y/Y) misses by $50M.

data.title: Silicon Motion announces $50M buyback program
data.description:
• Silicon Motion Technology (NASDAQ:SIMO) said that its Board of Directors has authorized a new share repurchase program and approved related cash disbursement for the company to repurchase up to $50M of its ADSs over a six-month period, effective immediately.
  • As of December 31, 2024, the company had approximately $334.3M of cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash and short-term investments.
  • "We experienced significant top-and-bottom-line growth in fiscal year 2024 as our strategy to capture greater market share and diversify our product portfolio and addressable markets is delivering results,” said Wallace Kou, President & CEO.
  • The stock price dipped 6.4% on Wednesday during after market hours of trading.

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims sank by 16,000 to a low 207,000 last week and are expected to rebound to 215,000 this week, back above their four-week moving average of 213,000.

8:30 AM Productivity and Costs

Productivity measures the growth of labor efficiency in producing the economy's goods and services.

The first reading on productivity for the fourth quarter is expected to show an annual gain of 1.8 percent compared with 2.2 percent in Q3.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

5:10 PM Fed's Logan Speech

Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan will participate in"Future Challenges for Monetary Policy in the Americas" panel before the Bank for International Settlements Chapultepec Conference.

7:30 PM Fed's Waller Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller will discuss "Payments" before the"Navigating the Future of Payments with Christopher Waller" event hosted by the Atlantic Council.
